mainmatter.p: If you have a LaTeX document which

ul.li: is large (probably several hundreds of pages at least)
needs to be externally referenced often

mainmatter.p: you will run into the problem that

ol.li: large PDFs are not easily navigable
PDFs of any size are not very searchable
the internal numbering changes often, making external references outdated

mainmatter.p: Gerby addresses these problems by providing an online tag-based view, instead of just having a big PDF. Gerby is tailored towards making large online textbooks and reference works more accessible.
In case you were wondering, a gerbe is a kind of stack (in the mathematical sense), and the software was originally meant for the Stacks project.
